workflow.name: Turn meeting transcripts or audio into Google Docs action summaries with Groq Whisper, OpenRouter, and Google Docs
workflow.description:
# Meeting Transcript → AI Summary & Google Doc (Text + Audio)

Turn raw meeting transcripts or audio recordings into structured, 
ready-to-share Google Docs — automatically. No manual note-taking, 
no copy-pasting.

## What It Does

Send a meeting transcript (text or audio file) via a webhook and this 
workflow will:

- Auto-detect whether you sent text or an audio file
- Transcribe audio using Groq Whisper (free — 28,800 sec/day)
- Use AI to extract a structured summary via OpenRouter
- Create a formatted Google Doc with 4 clearly defined sections:
  - 📌 Executive Summary (2–3 sentences)
  - ✅ Key Decisions Made
  - 📋 Action Items (with Owner & Deadline)
  - 🗓️ Suggested Next Meeting Agenda
- Return the Google Doc link instantly in the webhook response

## Who Is This For

- Teams and managers who want automatic meeting notes without extra tools
- Anyone recording calls or meetings who needs structured follow-ups fast
- Operations and project managers tracking decisions and action items

## How It Works

1. **Trigger** — Send a POST request to the webhook with either:
   - JSON body: `transcript`, `meeting_title`, `meeting_date`
   - Multipart form-data: `audio` file (.mp3, .mp4, .wav, .m4a)
2. **Route** — An If node detects whether text or audio was sent
3. **Transcribe** *(audio path only)* — Groq Whisper transcribes the audio
4. **Summarize** — OpenRouter AI (GPT-4o-mini) structures content into JSON
5. **Format** — A Code node builds the final document content
6. **Publish** — A Google Doc is created and the summary is written into it
7. **Respond** — Webhook returns the doc title, URL, and summary preview

## Requirements

| Service | Purpose | Cost |
|---|---|---|
| [Groq](https://console.groq.com) | Audio transcription (Whisper) | Free (28,800 sec/day) |
| [OpenRouter](https://openrouter.ai) | AI summarization | Free models available |
| Google Docs OAuth2 | Create & write documents | Free |

## Setup Steps

1. **Groq Credential**
   - Get a free API key at console.groq.com
   - In n8n → Settings → Credentials → New → **Header Auth**
   - Set Name: `Authorization`, Value: `Bearer YOUR_GROQ_KEY`
   - Select this credential in the **Transcribe Audio** node

2. **OpenRouter Credential**
   - Get a free API key at openrouter.ai
   - In n8n → Settings → Credentials → New → **Header Auth**
   - Set Name: `Authorization`, Value: `Bearer YOUR_OPENROUTER_KEY`
   - Select this credential in the **Call AI API** node

3. **Google Docs**
   - In n8n → Settings → Credentials → New → **Google Docs OAuth2**
   - Sign in and allow access
   - Select this credential in both Google Docs nodes

4. **Activate** the workflow and copy the webhook URL from the trigger node

## Example Request (Text)

POST to your webhook URL with:
```json
{
  "transcript": "John: Let's move the launch to Q3...",
  "meeting_title": "Q2 Planning Sync",
  "meeting_date": "2025-05-05"
}
```

## Example Request (Audio)

POST with `multipart/form-data`:
- `audio` → your audio file (.mp3, .mp4, .wav, .m4a)
- `meeting_title` → optional
- `meeting_date` → optional

parameters.content:
**🎙️ STEP 2 — GROQ SETUP (Audio Transcription)**

Free tier: 28,800 seconds/day — no card needed!

1. Go to console.groq.com → API Keys → Create Key
2. In n8n → Settings → Credentials → New → **Header Auth**
   - Name: `Authorization`
   - Value: `Bearer YOUR_GROQ_KEY_HERE`
3. In the **Transcribe Audio** node → select this credential

Supported formats: .mp3 .mp4 .wav .m4a .webm
Model: whisper-large-v3-turbo (fast + accurate)

**🤖 STEP 3 — OPENROUTER SETUP (AI Summarization)**

1. Go to openrouter.ai → Keys → Create Key
2. In n8n → Settings → Credentials → New → **Header Auth**
   - Name: `Authorization`
   - Value: `Bearer YOUR_OPENROUTER_KEY_HERE`
3. In the **Call AI API** node → select this credential

Free models available!
Recommended: openai/gpt-4o-mini

**📄 STEP 4 — GOOGLE DOCS SETUP**

1. In n8n → Settings → Credentials → New → Google Docs OAuth2
2. Sign in with your Google account and allow access
3. Select this credential in both Google Docs nodes

The summary doc will be created in your Google Drive root folder. You can move or rename it afterwards.

**⚙️ STEP 1 — TRIGGER SETUP**

**📝 Text Input:**
Send POST with JSON body:
```json
{
  "transcript": "Full meeting transcript...",
  "meeting_title": "Weekly Sync",
  "meeting_date": "2025-05-05"
}
```

**🎙️ Audio Input:**
Send POST with multipart/form-data:
- `audio` → your audio file
- `meeting_title` → optional
- `meeting_date` → optional

Workflow auto-detects which type was sent!

## 📋 Meeting Summary → Action Items 

**Who is this for?**
Anyone who wants to turn raw meeting transcripts OR audio recordings into structured, ready-to-use summaries — without manually writing notes.

**What it does:**
Send transcript text OR upload an audio file → AI generates a clean Google Doc with:
- ✅ Executive Summary (2–3 lines)
- ✅ Key Decisions Made
- ✅ Action Items (numbered, with Owner & Deadline)
- ✅ Suggested Next Meeting Agenda

**Supports Two Input Types:**
📝 Text — Paste transcript as JSON
🎙️ Audio — Upload .mp3 / .mp4 / .wav / .m4a (auto-transcribed via Groq Whisper)

**How it works:**
1. Send POST request with text OR audio file
2. Workflow auto-detects input type
3. Audio is transcribed via Groq Whisper (free, 28,800 sec/day)
4. AI structures content into 4 sections
5. Google Doc is created and link returned

**Requirements:**
- Groq API Key → stored as Header Auth credential (free)
- OpenRouter API Key → stored as Header Auth credential
- Google Docs OAuth2 credentials

user.name: SPCTEK AI
user.bio:
SPCTEK.AI is developing a suite of practical AI-powered tools and operational systems designed to help businesses understand, improve, and scale their processes.

Our focus is not on building complex platforms. We build solutions that are simple, usable, and directly connected to real business operations.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
